Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.1 verdict: HALT_Q20A_WASOW_PDF_IMAGE_ONLY. Wasow §X.3 theorems (i) Newton-polygon slope-p/q to rank-q irregular singularity, (ii) characteristic exponents = roots of degree-q polynomial, (iii) Borel-singularity radius = 1/|c_root| cannot be supported by AEAL evidence at this dispatch.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (Birkhoff §2): birkhoff_1930.pdf has machine-readable text layer. pypdf 6.x extract_text succeeds across all 42 pages. §2 ('Statement of the Formal Problem') spans paper pp.209-214 (text-dump pp.6-10).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i): Birkhoff 1930 §2 formal-series existence theorem extracted at p.214 (verbatim quote, 28 words): 'It is our primary purpose in Part I of this paper [...] by proving that there always exist precisely n formal series solutions of the three types (6), (6'), (6").' States existence uniformly in equation order n.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i): Birkhoff §2 Newton-polygon construction at p.211 (verbatim quote, 28 words): 'a unique broken line L, concave upwards, whose vertices fall at certain of these points, while all of the other points lie above or on this line L'. Construction is uniform in n (no cap on number of vertices k <= n).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i): Birkhoff §2 'basic integer' p (Puiseux denominator) at p.209 (verbatim quote, 24 words): 'all possible values of p are evidently positive integral multiples of a least value p_0 >= 1'. Statement is uniform in p (no cap).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i) verdict: C_BIRKHOFF_UNIFORM. Birkhoff 1930 §2 formal-series existence theorem applies uniformly in equation order n >= 1 and Puiseux denominator p >= 1. Specialised to PCF-1/PCF-2 (n = d, p = 1), this yields a uniform-in-d theorem-grade citation for the formal-direction half of PCF-2 v1.3 §3.3.A* (the xi_0(d) = d/beta_d^{1/d} claim).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (ii) verdict: C_BIRKHOFF_BOREL_NOT_IN_§§2-3. The Borel-transform leading-singularity claim (Prompt 018 §2 step 5 second bullet) is NOT in Birkhoff 1930 §§2-3. The word 'Borel' does not appear in the §§2-3 text dump. The relevant content lives elsewhere in the literature (likely Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933 or Wasow §X.3); spec error noted in unexpected_finds.json.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 §3 result: uniqueness of converse correspondence (formal series -> difference equation), p.214 (verbatim quote, 28 words): 'It is an easy matter to solve the converse problem by demonstrating that to every such set of n (linearly independent) formal series there corresponds a uniquely determined linear difference equation of the n-th order.' Uniform in n.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.3 verdict: C_LITERATURE_BLOCKED_AT_C1. Aggregate proof d-range = min(d_W*, d_B*, ∞) = undetermined because d_W* is unknown (Wasow blocked) and the Borel-half of d_B* is unknown (content not in Birkhoff §§2-3). Formal-half d-range from Birkhoff §2 is uniform (= ∞).

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow Theorem 11.1 (Chap IV §11, pg 52) extracted: formal-similarity result splitting two distinct eigenvalue groups, uniform in n. Vision-transcribed from PNG at SHA-256 of source PNG at literature/52.png.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow Theorem 12.1 (Chap IV §12, pp 56-58) extracted: Main Asymptotic Existence Theorem (distinct case). Hypothesis: q a nonnegative integer. Conclusion: x^{-q} Y' = A(x) Y has fundamental matrix solution analytic in any sector of central angle ≤ π/(q+1), asymptotic to formal solution. Uniform in n and q ≥ 0; no d-cap.
Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow Theorem 12.2 (Chap IV §12, pg 58) extracted: two-eigenvalue-group reduction matrix function P(x) holomorphic in S with asymptotic expansion. Uniform in n.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow pg 59 transition note extracted: 'The general case (with multiple eigenvalues) will be given in Chapter V, after the Jordan canonical form has been introduced and used.' Confirms Wasow §§16-19 contains the general case; dispatch 3 halt unblocked.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow Theorem 12.3 (Chap IV §12, referenced from §19.5 pg 111) extracted: distinct-case canonical form Y(x) = Ŷ(x) x^G exp[Q(x)] with Q diagonal polynomial of degree q+1, Ŷ(x) ~ formal series in x^{-1}. Uniform in n and q ≥ 0.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow §19.1 reduction lemma (Chap V §19.1, pg 100, eq. 19.1) extracted: x^{-q} Y' = A(x) Y where q is a nonnegative integer; A(x) holomorphic in sector S with asymptotic series in x^{-1}. q ≥ 0 uniform.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow eq. (19.3) (Chap V §19.1, pg 100) extracted: gauge transformation Y = Z exp[λ x^{q+1}/(q+1)] reduces (19.1) to x^{-q} Z' = (A(x) − λI) Z. Encodes Borel-singularity radius 1/|λ| at rank-q irregular singularity uniformly in q ≥ 0. RE-TARGETS Prompt 018 §2 step 5 (ii) Borel content from Birkhoff §§2-3 (where it was not present, dispatch 3 finding) to Wasow §19.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow §19.2 extension (Chap V §19.2, pg 102) extracted: Theorem 11.1 method extends to general (Jordan-block) case via reduction to block-diagonal form even with repeated eigenvalues. Uniform in n.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: Wasow §19.3 shearing transformation (Chap V §19.3, pp 104-110) extracted: Y = S(x) Y₁ with S(x) = diag(1, x^{-g}, …, x^{-(n-1)g}) reduces rank q iteratively. Independent-variable ramification x = const · t^p for integer p handles fractional q. Uniform in q ∈ ℚ_≥0.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1 KEYSTONE: Wasow Theorem 19.1 (Chap V §19.5, pg 111) extracted: Let A(x) be n-by-n matrix function holomorphic for |x| ≥ x₀, x ∈ S, with asymptotic series in x^{-1}. Then x^{-q} Y' = A(x) Y has a fundamental matrix solution Y(x) = Ŷ(x) x^G exp[Q(x)] on every sufficiently narrow subsector. Q(x) diagonal with diagonal polynomials in x^{1/p}, p positive integer; G constant; Ŷ admits asymptotic series in x^{-1/p}. Uniform in n, uniform in q ≥ 0; only new feature vs Thm 12.3 is Puiseux exponent 1/p determined by Jordan structure of A_0. NO d-cap, q-cap, or n-cap.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1: vocabulary equivalence judgment recorded. Wasow uses 'shearing transformations' + 'characteristic exponents'; Adams 1928 / Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933 / Birkhoff 1930 / Prompt 018 spec use 'Newton polygon edges' + 'characteristic roots'. The slope-1/d Newton-polygon edge corresponds to the smallest shearing exponent g₀ = 1/d in Wasow §19.3. Treated as substantively equivalent for verdict purposes.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.1 verdict: C_WASOW_UNIFORM. Theorem 19.1 plus eq. (19.3) cover the SIARC stratum at every d ≥ 2 uniformly via PCF d ↔ Wasow q correspondence q = (d+2)/2; fractional q handled by §19.3 ramification x = const · t^p with p = 2 sufficient for all d ≥ 2.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.2 carry-forward: Birkhoff §2 formal-series existence (dispatch 3 verdict C_BIRKHOFF_UNIFORM uniform in n=d) retained unchanged. Borel-half re-targeted from Birkhoff §§2-3 (not there per dispatch 3 finding) to Wasow §19 eq. (19.3) (per dispatch 4 Phase C.1 above). Carry-forward verdict signal: C_BIRKHOFF_UNIFORM (formal half) ∧ C_BOREL_VIA_WASOW_§19_UNIFORM (Borel half re-targeted).
Q20A dispatch 4 Phase C.3 verdict: C_LITERATURE_UNIFORM. Aggregate proof d-range = min(d_W*, d_B*-formal, d_B*-Borel-via-Wasow) = min(∞, ∞, ∞) = ∞. Proof is uniform in d ≥ 2 with no cap.

Q20A dispatch 4 Phase D verdict: UPGRADE_FULL.
